 {"id":"bd-1bruk","title":"PromptInjector: Support review-mode prompts for gmi agents","description":"## Purpose
Generate review-mode prompts that instruct agents to analyze and provide feedback on existing code rather than making changes, supporting quality review workflows.

## Implementation Details

```go
// ReviewPromptGenerator creates review-mode prompts for agents
type ReviewPromptGenerator struct {
    templates map[string]string // agent_type -> template
    logger    *slog.Logger
}

// Default review templates per agent type
var defaultReviewTemplates = map[string]string{
    "cc": `Review the code in this project. Focus on:
1. Potential bugs or edge cases
2. Security vulnerabilities
3. Performance concerns
4. Code clarity and maintainability

Do NOT make any changes. Only provide analysis and recommendations.`,
    
    "cod": `Analyze the codebase and identify:
- Code quality issues
- Missing error handling
- Test coverage gaps
- Documentation needs

Output findings only, no modifications.`,
    
    "gmi": `Perform a code review focusing on:
- Architecture patterns
- API design
- Dependency concerns
- Scalability considerations

Analysis mode only - no code changes.`,
}

// GenerateReviewPrompt creates a review prompt for the given context
func (g *ReviewPromptGenerator) GenerateReviewPrompt(agentType, projectPath string, opts ReviewOptions) string {
    template := g.templates[agentType]
    if template == "" {
        template = defaultReviewTemplates[agentType]
    }
    
    prompt := template
    if opts.FocusArea != "" {
        prompt = fmt.Sprintf("Focus specifically on: %s\n\n%s", opts.FocusArea, prompt)
    }
    if opts.FilePattern != "" {
        prompt = fmt.Sprintf("Review files matching: %s\n\n%s", opts.FilePattern, prompt)
    }
    
    g.logger.Info("generated review prompt",
        "agent_type", agentType,
        "project", filepath.Base(projectPath),
        "focus_area", opts.FocusArea,
        "file_pattern", opts.FilePattern,
        "prompt_len", len(prompt))
    
    return prompt
}

type ReviewOptions struct {
    FocusArea   string // e.g., "security", "performance"
    FilePattern string // e.g., "*.go", "internal/**"
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log prompt generation with agent type and project
- Log any customization options applied
- Log final prompt length (not content)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Distinct review templates per agent type
- [ ] Templates explicitly forbid code changes
- [ ] Support for focus area customization
- [ ] Support for file pattern scoping
- [ ] Custom template override capability
- [ ] Unit tests for each agent type template","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:44.480042400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:35:53.566912750Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:35:53.566865331Z","close_reason":"Complete: ReviewPromptGenerator implemented with all acceptance criteria met - distinct templates per agent type, no-changes enforcement, focus area/file pattern options, custom override capability, and comprehensive unit tests for all agent types.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bruk","depends_on_id":"bd-28cpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bruk","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1bz6d","title":"BeadScanner: Execute br list --status open --json per project","description":"## BeadScanner: Execute br list --status open --json per project

### Purpose
Implement the actual bead counting via br CLI subprocess calls.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/bead_scanner.go`

**br CLI Integration:**
```go
// countBeads runs br list and parses the JSON output.
func (s *BeadScanner) countBeads(ctx context.Context, projectPath string) (int, error) {
    // Build command: br list --status open --json
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, s.brPath, "list", "--status", "open", "--json")
    cmd.Dir = projectPath
    
    // Capture output
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        // br might not be installed or project has no .beads
        if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
            // Non-zero exit is OK - might just mean no beads
            log.Debug("[BeadScanner] br_exit_error", 
                "project", projectPath, 
                "code", exitErr.ExitCode())
        }
        return 0, nil // Treat as 0 beads, not an error
    }
    
    // Parse JSON array
    var beads []json.RawMessage
    if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &beads); err != nil {
        // Might be empty or malformed
        log.Warn("[BeadScanner] json_parse_error", 
            "project", projectPath, 
            "error", err)
        return 0, nil
    }
    
    return len(beads), nil
}
```

**Expected br Output:**
```json
[
  {"id": "bd-xxx", "title": "...", "status": "open", ...},
  {"id": "bd-yyy", "title": "...", "status": "open", ...}
]
```

**Parallel Scanning:**
```go
// scanAll scans all projects concurrently.
func (s *BeadScanner) scanAll(ctx context.Context, projects []string) []ProjectBeadCount {
    results := make([]ProjectBeadCount, len(projects))
    var wg sync.WaitGroup
    sem := make(chan struct{}, s.Parallelism) // Limit concurrency
    
    for i, project := range projects {
        wg.Add(1)
        go func(idx int, proj string) {
            defer wg.Done()
            sem <- struct{}{}        // Acquire
            defer func() { <-sem }() // Release
            
            count, err := s.countBeads(ctx, proj)
            if err != nil {
                log.Warn("[BeadScanner] scan_error", "project", proj, "error", err)
            }
            
            results[idx] = ProjectBeadCountFromPath(proj, count)
            log.Info("[BeadScanner] project_scanned", "project", proj, "beads", count)
        }(i, project)
    }
    
    wg.Wait()
    return results
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log br call: `[BeadScanner] br_call project=%s`
- Log br result: `[BeadScanner] br_result project=%s beads=%d`
- Log br error: `[BeadScanner] br_error project=%s error=%v`
- Log parse error: `[BeadScanner] json_parse_error project=%s`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Execute br list --status open --json
- [ ] Parse JSON array and count elements
- [ ] Handle br not installed gracefully
- [ ] Handle projects without .beads gracefully
- [ ] Concurrent scanning with configurable parallelism","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:59:34.093978854Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:29:56.644658245Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:29:56.640839271Z","close_reason":"Implemented in bead_scanner.go - countBeads function uses br list --status open --json with concurrent scanning, JSON parsing, and graceful error handling","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bz6d","depends_on_id":"bd-2xinm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bz6d","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1c6d7","title":"DCG: Implement send command interception for non-Claude agents","description":"Intercept 'ntm send' commands and run through DCG before forwarding to pane.

## Why Needed

Codex and Gemini agents don't have PreToolUse hooks like Claude Code.
We need to intercept at the NTM level when sending commands to their panes.

## Implementation

### In ntm send command:
1. Before sending to pane, check if target is non-Claude agent
2. If DCG enabled, extract command portion from send payload
3. Run: dcg check --command "<extracted_command>"
4. If dcg returns non-zero, block send and return error
5. If dcg returns zero, proceed with normal send

### Challenges
- Identifying "command" content in arbitrary send payloads
- Some sends are just text, not commands
- Need heuristic: if starts with common command patterns, check DCG

### Heuristics for command detection
- Starts with: git, rm, mv, cp, chmod, chown, kubectl, terraform
- Contains: sudo, -rf, --force, --hard
- Looks like shell: contains |, &&, ||, ;

### Code Location
- internal/commands/send.go (add DCG check)
- internal/integrations/dcg/check.go (dcg invocation wrapper)

## Testing
- Send safe command to Codex pane, verify passes
- Send dangerous command to Codex pane, verify blocked
- Send arbitrary text (not command), verify passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:10.955702295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:21.649341702Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:44:21.649289824Z","close_reason":"Implemented DCG interception in send.go with command extraction heuristics and audit logging","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c6d7","depends_on_id":"bd-1m6sw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1c6d7","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1c6d7","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1c9ib","title":"RCH Integration: Remote Compilation Helper for Build Offloading","description":"Integrate RCH (remote_compilation_helper) to offload compilation to remote workers.

## Project Location
/dp/remote_compilation_helper (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Multi-agent workflows often trigger parallel builds:
- Agent 1: cargo build on project A
- Agent 2: go build on project B
- Agent 3: cargo test on project A

This causes:
- CPU saturation (all cores maxed)
- Thermal throttling
- Slow agent response times
- Failed builds due to resource contention

## What RCH Does

RCH transparently offloads compilation to remote workers:

### Architecture
1. PreToolUse hook intercepts cargo/go build commands
2. RCH client checks for available workers
3. If worker available, rsync project and run remotely
4. Stream output back to local terminal
5. Rsync artifacts back when done

### Key Features
- Transparent: no agent changes needed
- Deduplication: same build on same worker reuses cache
- Fallback: if no workers, run locally
- Worker fleet: multiple machines can serve as workers

### Key RCH Commands
```bash
rch status            # Show worker status
rch workers list      # List available workers
rch build cargo       # Explicit remote build
rch config            # Configure workers
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. PreToolUse Hook (Primary)
Configure hook for all agents to route builds through RCH.

### 2. Dashboard Widget
Show compilation status: local vs remote builds, worker utilization, time saved.

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-rch-status: Get RCH and worker status
- --robot-rch-workers: List workers

## Considerations
- Network latency: remote only wins for long builds (>10s)
- Security: workers must be trusted (code execution)
- Artifacts: some builds produce local-only files
- DCG: RCH commands should be whitelisted

## Acceptance Criteria

1. RCH integration module exists at internal/integrations/rch/
2. PreToolUse hook configuration for cargo/go build interception works
3. Dashboard widget shows local vs remote build stats
4. Robot mode commands return valid JSON with worker status
5. Fallback to local build works when no workers available
6. All child beads (bd-1j956, bd-64v8u, bd-1o1q1, bd-398bh, bd-3u84u, bd-1ghcp) completed

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/integrations/rch/rch_test.go
- Test worker detection, build routing, fallback logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RCH] RCH Integration Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RCH]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing RCH integration"
# Test robot-rch-status command
ntm --robot-rch-status | jq -e '.rch_installed \!= null' || exit 1
echo "$LOG_PREFIX RCH status check passed"

package agent

import (

    "testing"

func loadTestData(t *testing.T, filename string) string {
    t.Helper()
    data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join("testdata", filename))
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Failed to load test data %s: %v", filename, err)
    }
    return string(data)
}

func TestParser_ClaudeCode_Working(t *testing.T) {
    parser := NewParser()
    output := loadTestData(t, "cc_working.txt")
    
    state, err := parser.Parse(output)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Parse failed: %v", err)
    }
    
    // Assertions
    if state.Type != AgentTypeClaudeCode {
        t.Errorf("Expected AgentTypeClaudeCode, got %v", state.Type)
    }
    if !state.IsWorking {
        t.Error("Expected IsWorking=true for active Claude Code")
    }
    if state.IsIdle {
        t.Error("Expected IsIdle=false for active Claude Code")
    }
    if state.GetRecommendation() != RecommendDoNotInterrupt {
        t.Errorf("Expected DO_NOT_INTERRUPT, got %v", state.GetRecommendation())
    }
}

func TestParser_Codex_ContextPercentage(t *testing.T) {
    parser := NewParser()
    output := loadTestData(t, "cod_idle.txt")
    
    state, err := parser.Parse(output)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Parse failed: %v", err)
    }
    
    if state.Type != AgentTypeCodex {
        t.Errorf("Expected AgentTypeCodex, got %v", state.Type)
    }
    if state.ContextRemaining == nil {
        t.Fatal("Expected ContextRemaining to be set for Codex")
    }
    // Test data has "47% context left"
    if *state.ContextRemaining != 47.0 {
        t.Errorf("Expected 47.0%% context, got %.1f%%", *state.ContextRemaining)
    }
}

func TestParser_Codex_LowContext(t *testing.T) {
    parser := NewParserWithConfig(ParserConfig{
        ContextLowThreshold: 20.0,
        SampleLength:        500,
    })
    
    // Simulate output with "12% context left"
    output := "12% context left · ? for shortcuts\n>"
    
    state, err := parser.Parse(output)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("Parse failed: %v", err)
    }
    
    if !state.IsContextLow {
        t.Error("Expected IsContextLow=true for 12% context")
    }
}

func TestParser_RateLimit_AllAgents(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        file     string
        expected AgentType
    }{
        {"Claude Code rate limit", "cc_ratelimit.txt", AgentTypeClaudeCode},
        {"Codex rate limit", "cod_ratelimit.txt", AgentTypeCodex},
    }
    
    parser := NewParser()
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            output := loadTestData(t, tt.file)
            state, err := parser.Parse(output)
            if err != nil {
                t.Fatalf("Parse failed: %v", err)
            }
            
            if !state.IsRateLimited {
                t.Error("Expected IsRateLimited=true")
            }
            if state.GetRecommendation() != RecommendRateLimitedWait {
                t.Errorf("Expected RATE_LIMITED_WAIT, got %v", state.GetRecommendation())
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestParser_Recommendation_Priority(t *testing.T) {
    // Test that rate limit takes precedence over working
    state := &AgentState{
        IsWorking:     true,
        IsRateLimited: true,
    }
    
    // Rate limit should win
    if state.GetRecommendation() != RecommendRateLimitedWait {
        t.Errorf("Rate limit should take precedence, got %v", state.GetRecommendation())
    }
}

func TestParser_Confidence_Codex(t *testing.T) {
    parser := NewParser()
    
    // Codex with explicit percentage should have high confidence
    output := "47% context left · ? for shortcuts"
    state, _ := parser.Parse(output)
    
    if state.Confidence < 0.7 {

func TestParser_EmptyOutput(t *testing.T) {
    parser := NewParser()

}

Implement `ntm handoff` command for manual handoff creation.

## Background & Reasoning
While auto-handoffs capture basic state, manual handoffs allow agents/users to:
1. Add detailed reasoning and decisions
2. Explicitly state what comes next
3. Record findings that aren't in output
4. Create clean handoff points at logical boundaries

## Command Interface
```bash
# Create handoff for current/specified session
ntm handoff [session]

# Create with message (skip interactive prompt)
ntm handoff myproject --goal "Completed auth" --now "Add tests"

# Create with full details from file
ntm handoff myproject --from-file handoff.yaml

# List recent handoffs
ntm handoff list [session]

# Show specific handoff
ntm handoff show <path>
```

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/cli/handoff.go
```go
package cli

import (
    "bufio"
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "os"
    "strings"

    "github.com/spf13/cobra"
    "github.com/yourusername/ntm/internal/handoff"
)

var handoffCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "handoff [session]",
    Short: "Create or manage session handoffs",
    Long:  `Create, list, or view handoffs for preserving context across sessions.`,
}

var handoffCreateCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "create [session]",
    Short: "Create a new handoff",
    RunE:  runHandoffCreate,
}

var handoffListCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "list [session]",
    Short: "List handoffs for a session",
    RunE:  runHandoffList,
}

var handoffShowCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "show <path>",
    Short: "Show a specific handoff",
    RunE:  runHandoffShow,
}

func init() {
    rootCmd.AddCommand(handoffCmd)
    handoffCmd.AddCommand(handoffCreateCmd)
    handoffCmd.AddCommand(handoffListCmd)
    handoffCmd.AddCommand(handoffShowCmd)

    handoffCreateCmd.Flags().String("goal", "", "What this session accomplished")
    handoffCreateCmd.Flags().String("now", "", "What next session should do first")
    handoffCreateCmd.Flags().String("from-file", "", "Create from YAML file")
    handoffCreateCmd.Flags().Bool("auto", false, "Generate from agent output")
    handoffCreateCmd.Flags().String("description", "", "Short description for filename")
    handoffCreateCmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON")

    handoffListCmd.Flags().Int("limit", 10, "Maximum number of handoffs to list")
    handoffListCmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON")

    handoffShowCmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON")
}

func runHandoffCreate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    sessionName := getSessionName(args)
    goal, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("goal")
    now, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("now")
    fromFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from-file")
    auto, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("auto")
    description, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("description")
    jsonOutput, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")

    slog.Debug("handoff create command",
        "session", sessionName,
        "goal_provided", goal != "",
        "now_provided", now != "",
        "from_file", fromFile,
        "auto", auto,
    )

    writer := handoff.NewWriter(projectDir)
    var h *handoff.Handoff

    if fromFile != "" {
        // Load from file
        reader := handoff.NewReader(projectDir)
        var err error
        h, err = reader.Read(fromFile)
        if err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to read handoff file: %w", err)
        }
    } else if auto {
        // Auto-generate from agent output
        generator := handoff.NewGenerator(projectDir)
        var err error
        h, err = generator.GenerateFromOutput(sessionName, nil) // Would need actual output
        if err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("auto-generation failed: %w", err)
        }
    } else if goal == "" || now == "" {
        // Interactive mode
        var err error
        h, err = runInteractiveHandoff(sessionName)
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
    } else {
        // Use provided flags
        h = &handoff.Handoff{
            Session: sessionName,
            Goal:    goal,
            Now:     now,
            Status:  handoff.StatusComplete,
        }
    }

    // Validate
    if errs := h.Validate(); len(errs) > 0 {
        return fmt.Errorf("validation failed: %v", errs[0])
    }

    // Determine description for filename
    if description == "" {
        description = generateDescription(h.Goal)
    }

    // Write handoff
    path, err := writer.Write(h, description)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to write handoff: %w", err)
    }

    slog.Info("handoff created",
        "path", path,
        "session", sessionName,
        "goal", truncate(h.Goal, 50),
    )

    if jsonOutput {
        return outputJSON(map[string]interface{}{
            "success": true,
            "path":    path,
            "session": sessionName,
            "goal":    h.Goal,
            "now":     h.Now,
        })
    }

    fmt.Printf("Handoff created: %s\n", path)
    return nil
}

### 2. Interactive Mode
When --goal and --now not provided, prompt interactively:
```go
func runInteractiveHandoff(sessionName string) (*handoff.Handoff, error) {
    reader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)
    h := &handoff.Handoff{Session: sessionName}

    fmt.Printf("Creating handoff for session: %s\n\n", sessionName)

    // Goal (required)
    fmt.Print("What did this session accomplish?\n> ")
    goal, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
    h.Goal = strings.TrimSpace(goal)
    if h.Goal == "" {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("goal is required")
    }

    // Now (required)
    fmt.Print("\nWhat should the next session do first?\n> ")
    now, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
    h.Now = strings.TrimSpace(now)
    if h.Now == "" {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("now is required")
    }

    // Blockers (optional)
    fmt.Print("\nAny blockers? (comma-separated, or empty)\n> ")
    blockersStr, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
    blockersStr = strings.TrimSpace(blockersStr)
    if blockersStr != "" {
        h.Blockers = strings.Split(blockersStr, ",")
        for i := range h.Blockers {
            h.Blockers[i] = strings.TrimSpace(h.Blockers[i])
        }
    }

    // Decisions (optional)
    fmt.Print("\nKey decisions made? (key=value, comma-separated, or empty)\n> ")
    decisionsStr, _ := reader.ReadString('\n')
    decisionsStr = strings.TrimSpace(decisionsStr)
    if decisionsStr != "" {
        h.Decisions = make(map[string]string)
        for _, kv := range strings.Split(decisionsStr, ",") {
            parts := strings.SplitN(strings.TrimSpace(kv), "=", 2)
            if len(parts) == 2 {
                h.Decisions[strings.TrimSpace(parts[0])] = strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])
            }
        }
    }

    // Status
    h.Status = handoff.StatusComplete
    if len(h.Blockers) > 0 {
        h.Status = handoff.StatusBlocked
    }

    return h, nil
}

func runHandoffList(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    sessionName := getSessionName(args)
    limit, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("limit")
    jsonOutput, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")

    reader := handoff.NewReader(projectDir)
    metas, err := reader.ListHandoffs(sessionName)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to list handoffs: %w", err)
    }

    if len(metas) > limit {
        metas = metas[:limit]
    }

    slog.Debug("listed handoffs",
        "session", sessionName,
        "count", len(metas),
    )

    if jsonOutput {
        return outputJSON(map[string]interface{}{
            "session":  sessionName,
            "count":    len(metas),
            "handoffs": metas,
        })
    }

    if len(metas) == 0 {
        fmt.Printf("No handoffs found for session: %s\n", sessionName)
        return nil
    }

    fmt.Printf("Session: %s (%d handoffs)\n\n", sessionName, len(metas))
    for _, m := range metas {
        autoTag := ""
        if m.IsAuto {
            autoTag = " [auto]"
        }
        fmt.Printf("  %s  %s%s\n", m.Date.Format("2006-01-19 15:04"), filepath.Base(m.Path), autoTag)
        fmt.Printf("    Goal: %s\n", truncate(m.Goal, 60))
        fmt.Printf("    Status: %s\n\n", m.Status)
    }

    return nil
}

### 5. Show Output
```go
func runHandoffShow(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    if len(args) < 1 {
        return fmt.Errorf("path argument required")
    }
    path := args[0]
    jsonOutput, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")

    reader := handoff.NewReader(projectDir)
    h, err := reader.Read(path)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to read handoff: %w", err)
    }

    if jsonOutput {
        return outputJSON(h)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Handoff: %s\n", path)
    fmt.Printf("Session: %s\n", h.Session)
    fmt.Printf("Date: %s\n", h.Date)
    fmt.Printf("Status: %s\n\n", h.Status)

    fmt.Printf("Goal: %s\n", h.Goal)
    fmt.Printf("Now: %s\n", h.Now)

    if h.Test != "" {
        fmt.Printf("Test: %s\n", h.Test)
    }

    if len(h.Blockers) > 0 {
        fmt.Printf("\nBlockers:\n")
        for _, b := range h.Blockers {
            fmt.Printf("  - %s\n", b)
        }
    }

    if len(h.Decisions) > 0 {
        fmt.Printf("\nDecisions:\n")
        for k, v := range h.Decisions {
            fmt.Printf("  %s: %s\n", k, v)
        }
    }

    if len(h.Next) > 0 {
        fmt.Printf("\nNext Steps:\n")
        for i, s := range h.Next {
            fmt.Printf("  %d. %s\n", i+1, s)
        }
    }

    return nil
}

### Purpose
Implement the PromptInjector that sends marching orders to agents. Handles staggered sending to avoid rate limits and agent-specific quirks.

### IMPORTANT: Check Existing Code
NTM already has `internal/session/prompts.go` and `internal/cli/send.go`. PromptInjector should:
1. Review existing send implementation

### Implementation Details

**File:** `internal/swarm/prompt_injector.go`

**Interface:**
```go
// PromptInjector sends prompts to agent panes.
type PromptInjector struct {
    // Config holds prompt templates and timing
    Config *config.SwarmConfig
    
    // tmuxClient for SendKeys
    tmuxClient *tmux.Client
    
    // StaggerDelay between sends (default 300ms)
    StaggerDelay time.Duration
}

// InjectionResult tracks a prompt injection.
type InjectionResult struct {
    SessionPane string        `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string        `json:"agent_type"`
    Success     bool          `json:"success"`
    Duration    time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    Error       string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
    SentAt      time.Time     `json:"sent_at"`
}

// BatchResult tracks batch injection.
type BatchResult struct {
    TotalPanes int               `json:"total_panes"`
    Successful int               `json:"successful"`
    Failed     int               `json:"failed"`
    Results    []InjectionResult `json:"results"`
    Duration   time.Duration     `json:"duration"`
}
```

**Core Methods:**
```go
// InjectPrompt sends a prompt to a single pane.
func (p *PromptInjector) InjectPrompt(sessionPane, agentType, prompt string) (*InjectionResult, error)

// InjectBatch sends prompts to multiple panes with staggering.
func (p *PromptInjector) InjectBatch(targets []InjectionTarget, prompt string) (*BatchResult, error)

// InjectSwarm sends marching orders to all panes in SwarmPlan.
func (p *PromptInjector) InjectSwarm(plan *SwarmPlan, prompt string) (*BatchResult, error)

// GetTemplate returns the prompt template by name (default, review, etc).
func (p *PromptInjector) GetTemplate(name string) string
```

**Agent-Specific Send Handling:**
```go
func (p *PromptInjector) sendToPane(sessionPane, agentType, prompt string) error {
    // Escape special characters in prompt
    escaped := escapeForTmux(prompt)
    
    // Send prompt text
    if err := p.tmuxClient.SendKeys(sessionPane, escaped, false); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("send prompt: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Send Enter to submit
    if err := p.tmuxClient.SendKeys(sessionPane, "", true); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("send enter: %w", err)
    }
    
    // CODEX QUIRK: Needs double Enter
    if agentType == "cod" {
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
        if err := p.tmuxClient.SendKeys(sessionPane, "", true); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("send second enter: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    return nil
}
```

 {"id":"bd-25wul","title":"Task: Implement ensemble state persistence and recovery","description":"# Task: Implement Ensemble State Persistence and Recovery

## Location
internal/ensemble/state.go
internal/state/ensemble_store.go

## Purpose
Persist ensemble session state so it survives process restarts and can be queried/resumed.

## State Storage

### Database Schema (SQLite)
```sql
CREATE TABLE ensemble_sessions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    session_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    question TEXT NOT NULL,
    preset_used TEXT,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    synthesis_strategy TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    synthesized_at TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (session_name) REFERENCES sessions(name)
);

CREATE TABLE mode_assignments (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    ensemble_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    mode_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    pane_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    agent_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    output_path TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (ensemble_id) REFERENCES ensemble_sessions(id)
);
```

### State Store Methods
```go
type EnsembleStore struct {
    db *sql.DB
}

func (s *EnsembleStore) SaveEnsemble(e *EnsembleSession) error { ... }

func (s *EnsembleStore) UpdateStatus(sessionName string, status EnsembleStatus) error { ... }

func (s *EnsembleStore) ListEnsembles() ([]*EnsembleSession, error) { ... }
func (s *EnsembleStore) DeleteEnsemble(sessionName string) error { ... }
```

### Recovery Scenarios
1. **Process restart**: Load ensemble state from DB, verify tmux session exists
2. **Partial spawn failure**: Track which assignments succeeded, allow retry
3. **Agent crash**: Detect via health check, optionally respawn with same mode
4. **Session detach/reattach**: State persists, can query via CLI/robot

+// runEnsembleUXCmd executes an ntm command and returns structured output.
+func runEnsembleUXCmd(t *testing.T, suite *TestSuite, label string, args ...string) cliRunResult {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "ntm", args...)
+	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
+
+	start := time.Now()
+	err := cmd.Run()
+	duration := time.Since(start)
+
+	if ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded && err == nil {
+		err = fmt.Errorf("command timed out after 60s")
+	}
+
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] %s args=%v duration_ms=%d err=%v", label, args, duration.Milliseconds(), err)
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] %s stdout=%s", label, strings.TrimSpace(stdout.String()))
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] %s stderr=%s", label, strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String()))
+
+	return cliRunResult{
+		Stdout:   stdout.Bytes(),
+		Stderr:   stderr.Bytes(),
+		Duration: duration,
+		Err:      err,
+	}
+}
+
+// parseEnsembleUXJSON unmarshals JSON output, fataling on error.
+func parseEnsembleUXJSON(t *testing.T, suite *TestSuite, label string, stdout []byte, v interface{}) {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(stdout, v); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] %s JSON parse failed: %v stdout=%s", label, err, string(stdout))
+	}
+	suite.Logger().LogJSON("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] "+label+" parsed", v)
+}

+func TestE2E_Suggest_KnownPatterns(t *testing.T) {

+	questions := []struct {

+		{"Is our API design correct for pagination?", "api_question"},
+		{"What are the security risks in our deployment?", "security_question"},
+	}
+
+	for _, q := range questions {
+		t.Run(q.label, func(t *testing.T) {
+			t.Logf("E2E: %s - suggesting preset for: %s", t.Name(), q.question)
+
+			res := runEnsembleUXCmd(t, suite, q.label,
+				fmt.Sprintf("--robot-ensemble-suggest=%s", q.question),

+func TestE2E_Estimate_BudgetWarning(t *testing.T) {
+	CommonE2EPrerequisites(t)
+
+	if !supportsNTMSubcommand("ensemble") {
+		t.Skip("ntm binary does not support `ensemble` command")
+	}
+
+	suite := NewTestSuite(t, "ensemble_ux_estimate_budget")
+	defer suite.Teardown()
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing estimate with tight budget", t.Name())
+
+	// Use all modes with a very tight budget to trigger a warning.
+	res := runEnsembleUXCmd(t, suite, "estimate_budget",
+		"ensemble", "estimate",
+		"--preset", "quick-scan",
+		"--question", "E2E estimate budget warning test",
+		"--budget-total", "100",
+		"--format", "json",
+	)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - stdout: %s", t.Name(), string(res.Stdout))
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - exit code err: %v", t.Name(), res.Err)
+
+	// Command may exit 0 with over_budget=true, or exit 1 with budget error.
+	// Either is acceptable; we just need structured output.
+	var estimate map[string]interface{}
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(res.Stdout, &estimate); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] estimate budget output not valid JSON: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	overBudget, _ := estimate["over_budget"].(bool)
+	warnings, hasWarnings := estimate["warnings"]
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: over_budget = %v, has_warnings = %v", t.Name(), overBudget, hasWarnings)
+
+	// With a 100-token budget, we expect over_budget or warnings.
+	if !overBudget && !hasWarnings {
+		t.Logf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] WARN: expected over_budget or warnings with 100 token budget; got neither")
+	}
+	if hasWarnings {
+		t.Logf("E2E: %s - warnings: %v", t.Name(), warnings)
+	}
+}

+				CompletedAt: &now,

+func TestE2E_Resume_AfterFailure(t *testing.T) {
+	SkipIfShort(t)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing checkpoint resume after simulated failure", t.Name())
+
+	// Use temp directory for checkpoint store.
+	tmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ntm-e2e-checkpoint-*")
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] failed to create temp dir: %v", err)
+	}
+	t.Cleanup(func() { os.RemoveAll(tmpDir) })
+
+	store, err := ensemble.NewCheckpointStore(tmpDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] NewCheckpointStore failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	runID := fmt.Sprintf("e2e-resume-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
+	now := time.Now().UTC()
+
+	// Save metadata with partial completion.
+	meta := ensemble.CheckpointMetadata{
+		SessionName:  "e2e-resume-session",
+		Question:     "E2E resume test",
+		RunID:        runID,
+		Status:       ensemble.EnsembleActive,
+		CreatedAt:    now,
+		UpdatedAt:    now,
+		CompletedIDs: []string{"mode-a"},
+		PendingIDs:   []string{"mode-b", "mode-c"},
+		ErrorIDs:     []string{},
+		TotalModes:   3,
+	}
+	if err := store.SaveMetadata(meta); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] SaveMetadata failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Save completed checkpoint for mode-a.
+	checkpoint := ensemble.ModeCheckpoint{
+		ModeID: "mode-a",
+		Output: &ensemble.ModeOutput{
+			ModeID:      "mode-a",
+			Thesis:      "Mode A completed thesis",
+			TopFindings: []ensemble.Finding{{Finding: "Finding A1", Impact: ensemble.ImpactMedium, Confidence: 0.8}},
+		},
+		Status:     "done",
+		CapturedAt: now,
+		TokensUsed: 500,
+	}
+	if err := store.SaveCheckpoint(runID, checkpoint); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] SaveCheckpoint failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify resume is possible.
+	manager := ensemble.NewCheckpointManager(store, runID)
+	resumable := manager.IsResumable()
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: resumable = %v (want true)", t.Name(), resumable)
+	if !resumable {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected checkpoint to be resumable")
+	}
+
+	// Get resume state.
+	resumeMeta, outputs, err := manager.GetResumeState()
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] GetResumeState failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: completed_ids = %v (want [mode-a])", t.Name(), resumeMeta.CompletedIDs)
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: pending_ids = %v (want [mode-b, mode-c])", t.Name(), resumeMeta.PendingIDs)
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: outputs count = %d (want 1)", t.Name(), len(outputs))
+
+	if len(resumeMeta.CompletedIDs) != 1 || resumeMeta.CompletedIDs[0] != "mode-a" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] unexpected completed_ids: %v", resumeMeta.CompletedIDs)
+	}
+	if len(resumeMeta.PendingIDs) != 2 {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] unexpected pending_ids: %v", resumeMeta.PendingIDs)
+	}
+	if len(outputs) != 1 {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected 1 completed output, got %d", len(outputs))
+	}
+}

+func TestE2E_RerunMode_Single(t *testing.T) {
+	SkipIfShort(t)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing single mode checkpoint save and reload", t.Name())
+
+	tmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ntm-e2e-rerun-*")
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] failed to create temp dir: %v", err)

+

+	runID := fmt.Sprintf("e2e-rerun-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())

+
+	// Save a checkpoint with an error.

+		ModeID:     modeID,
+		Status:     "error",
+		CapturedAt: now,

+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] SaveCheckpoint (error) failed: %v", err)

+
+	// Load and verify the error state.
+	loaded, err := store.LoadCheckpoint(runID, modeID)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] LoadCheckpoint failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: loaded.Status = %q (want error)", t.Name(), loaded.Status)
+	if loaded.Status != "error" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected error status, got %q", loaded.Status)
+	}
+
+	// Now save a successful rerun.
+	successCheckpoint := ensemble.ModeCheckpoint{
+		ModeID: modeID,
+		Output: &ensemble.ModeOutput{
+			ModeID: modeID,
+			Thesis: "Successful rerun thesis",
+			TopFindings: []ensemble.Finding{
+				{Finding: "Rerun finding", Impact: ensemble.ImpactLow, Confidence: 0.75},

+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] SaveCheckpoint (success) failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify the rerun replaced the error checkpoint.
+	reloaded, err := store.LoadCheckpoint(runID, modeID)

+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] LoadCheckpoint after rerun failed: %v", err)

+	if reloaded.Status != "done" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected done status after rerun, got %q", reloaded.Status)
+	}
+	if reloaded.Output == nil || reloaded.Output.Thesis != "Successful rerun thesis" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] rerun output not preserved")
+	}
+}

+	}

+
+	chunks, errCh := synthesizer.StreamSynthesize(ctx, input)
+
+	var received []ensemble.SynthesisChunk
+	for chunk := range chunks {
+		received = append(received, chunk)

+	}
+
+	if err := <-errCh; err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] StreamSynthesize error: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: chunk_count = %d (want >= 1)", t.Name(), len(received))
+	if len(received) == 0 {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected at least 1 synthesis chunk")

+
+	// Verify we got a complete chunk.
+	hasComplete := false
+	for _, chunk := range received {
+		if chunk.Type == ensemble.ChunkComplete {
+			hasComplete = true
+			break
+		}
+	}
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: has_complete_chunk = %v (want true)", t.Name(), hasComplete)
+	if !hasComplete {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected a ChunkComplete in stream")
+	}

+func TestE2E_Stream_CancelResume(t *testing.T) {
+	SkipIfShort(t)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing synthesis stream cancellation", t.Name())
+
+	cfg := ensemble.SynthesisConfig{
+		Strategy:      ensemble.StrategyManual,
+		MinConfidence: 0.3,
+		MaxFindings:   10,
+	}
+
+	synthesizer, err := ensemble.NewSynthesizer(cfg)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] NewSynthesizer failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	input := &ensemble.SynthesisInput{
+		Outputs: []ensemble.ModeOutput{
+			{
+				ModeID:      "cancel-mode",
+				Thesis:      "Cancel test thesis",
+				TopFindings: []ensemble.Finding{{Finding: "Cancel finding", Impact: ensemble.ImpactLow, Confidence: 0.5}},
+			},
+		},
+		OriginalQuestion: "E2E cancel resume test",
+		Config:           cfg,
+	}
+
+	// Start with a context we can cancel.
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
+
+	chunks, errCh := synthesizer.StreamSynthesize(ctx, input)
+
+	// Drain at least 1 chunk then cancel.
+	first, ok := <-chunks
+	if ok {
+		t.Logf("E2E: %s - received first chunk: type=%s", t.Name(), first.Type)
+		cancel()
+	} else {
+		cancel()
+		// Stream completed before we could cancel. That's fine.
+		t.Logf("E2E: %s - stream completed before cancel", t.Name())
+		<-errCh
+		return
+	}
+
+	// Drain remaining chunks after cancel.
+	remaining := 0
+	for range chunks {
+		remaining++
+	}
+
+	streamErr := <-errCh
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: remaining_chunks_after_cancel = %d, stream_err = %v", t.Name(), remaining, streamErr)
+
+	// After cancel, the error channel should either have a context.Canceled or nil.
+	// We don't fail on the specific error - just verify the stream didn't hang.
+}

+func TestE2E_Dedupe_Auto(t *testing.T) {
+	SkipIfShort(t)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing automatic finding deduplication", t.Name())
+
+	cfg := ensemble.DedupeConfig{
+		SimilarityThreshold:  0.7,
+		EvidenceWeight:       0.3,
+		TextWeight:           0.7,
+		PreferHighConfidence: true,
+		PreserveProvenance:   true,
+	}
+
+	engine := ensemble.NewDedupeEngine(cfg)
+
+	// Create overlapping findings from different modes as ModeOutput slices.
+	outputs := []ensemble.ModeOutput{
+		{
+			ModeID: "mode-security",
+			Thesis: "Security analysis",
+			TopFindings: []ensemble.Finding{
+				{Finding: "The authentication module has a SQL injection vulnerability", Impact: ensemble.ImpactCritical, Confidence: 0.95, EvidencePointer: "auth/login.go:42"},
+				{Finding: "Memory leak in the connection pool", Impact: ensemble.ImpactMedium, Confidence: 0.75, EvidencePointer: "pool/manager.go:105"},
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			ModeID: "mode-review",
+			Thesis: "Code review analysis",
+			TopFindings: []ensemble.Finding{
+				{Finding: "SQL injection risk in the auth login handler", Impact: ensemble.ImpactHigh, Confidence: 0.88, EvidencePointer: "auth/login.go:42"},
+				{Finding: "Connection pool leaks memory under load", Impact: ensemble.ImpactMedium, Confidence: 0.70, EvidencePointer: "pool/manager.go:110"},
+				{Finding: "Unused error return in file handler", Impact: ensemble.ImpactLow, Confidence: 0.60, EvidencePointer: "fs/handler.go:23"},
+			},
+		},
+	}
+
+	totalFindings := 0
+	for _, o := range outputs {
+		totalFindings += len(o.TopFindings)
+	}
+
+	result := engine.Dedupe(outputs)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: cluster_count = %d (want < %d input findings)", t.Name(), len(result.Clusters), totalFindings)
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - stats: input=%d clusters=%d", t.Name(), result.Stats.InputFindings, result.Stats.OutputClusters)
+
+	// With similar findings, we should see fewer clusters than input.
+	if len(result.Clusters) >= totalFindings {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected dedup to reduce findings: clusters=%d inputs=%d", len(result.Clusters), totalFindings)
+	}
+
+	// Log cluster details.
+	for i, cluster := range result.Clusters {
+		t.Logf("E2E: %s - cluster[%d]: canonical=%q confidence=%.2f members=%d",
+			t.Name(), i, truncateString(cluster.Canonical.Finding, 50), float64(cluster.MaxConfidence), cluster.MemberCount)
+	}
+}

+func TestE2E_ExportImport_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
+	SkipIfShort(t)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing export/import round-trip", t.Name())
+
+	// Get a real preset for the round-trip test.
+	engine := ensemble.NewSuggestionEngine()
+	presetNames := engine.ListPresets()
+	if len(presetNames) == 0 {
+		t.Skip("no presets available for export test")
+	}
+
+	preset := engine.GetPreset(presetNames[0])
+	if preset == nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] GetPreset(%q) returned nil", presetNames[0])
+	}
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - using preset %q for round-trip", t.Name(), preset.Name)
+
+	// Export from preset.
+	exported := ensemble.ExportFromPreset(*preset)
+
+	// Convert to JSON.
+	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(exported, "", "  ")
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] marshal export failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Write to temp file.
+	tmpFile := filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), fmt.Sprintf("ntm-e2e-export-%d.json", time.Now().UnixNano()))
+	if err := os.WriteFile(tmpFile, data, 0644); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] write export file failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	t.Cleanup(func() { os.Remove(tmpFile) })
+
+	// Read back and parse.
+	readData, err := os.ReadFile(tmpFile)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] read export file failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	var imported ensemble.EnsembleExport
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(readData, &imported); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] unmarshal import failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Convert back to preset.
+	roundTripped := imported.ToPreset()
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: roundTripped.Name = %q (want %q)", t.Name(), roundTripped.Name, preset.Name)
+	if roundTripped.Name != preset.Name {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] round-trip name mismatch: got %q want %q", roundTripped.Name, preset.Name)
+	}
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: roundTripped modes count = %d (want %d)", t.Name(), len(roundTripped.Modes), len(preset.Modes))
+	if len(roundTripped.Modes) != len(preset.Modes) {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] round-trip mode count mismatch: got %d want %d", len(roundTripped.Modes), len(preset.Modes))
+	}
+}

+func TestE2E_Import_RemoteRejected(t *testing.T) {
+	SkipIfShort(t)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing import validation rejects invalid data", t.Name())
+
+	// Create an invalid export with unknown mode references.
+	invalid := ensemble.EnsembleExport{
+		SchemaVersion: 1,
+		ExportedAt:    time.Now(),
+		Name:          "e2e-invalid-export",
+		DisplayName:   "Invalid Export",
+		Modes: []ensemble.ModeRef{
+			{ID: "nonexistent-mode-xyz", Code: "Z99"},
+		},
+	}
+
+	// Try converting and using the invalid export.
+	preset := invalid.ToPreset()
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: converted invalid preset name = %q", t.Name(), preset.Name)
+
+	// The preset should convert but modes reference will be unresolvable.
+	if len(preset.Modes) != 1 {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected 1 mode in invalid preset, got %d", len(preset.Modes))
+	}
+	if preset.Modes[0].ID != "nonexistent-mode-xyz" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected invalid mode ID preserved, got %q", preset.Modes[0].ID)
+	}
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: invalid mode ID preserved = true (want true)", t.Name())
+}

+func TestE2E_ExportFindings_DryRun(t *testing.T) {
+	CommonE2EPrerequisites(t)
+
+	if !supportsNTMSubcommand("ensemble") {
+		t.Skip("ntm binary does not support `ensemble` command")
+	}
+
+	suite := NewTestSuite(t, "ensemble_ux_export_findings_dryrun")
+	defer suite.Teardown()
+
+	if err := suite.Setup(); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] suite setup failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	session := suite.Session()
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing findings export dry-run", t.Name())
+
+	// Seed deterministic state.
+	now := time.Now().UTC()
+	panes, err := tmux.GetPanes(session)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] GetPanes failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	if len(panes) == 0 || panes[0].ID == "" {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] no panes available")
+	}
+	paneID := panes[0].ID
+
+	state := &ensemble.EnsembleSession{
+		SessionName:       session,
+		Question:          "E2E findings export dry-run test",
+		PresetUsed:        "e2e",
+		Status:            ensemble.EnsembleActive,
+		SynthesisStrategy: ensemble.StrategyManual,
+		CreatedAt:         now,
+		Assignments: []ensemble.ModeAssignment{
+			{
+				ModeID:      "e2e-export-mode",
+				PaneName:    paneID,
+				AgentType:   "cc",
+				Status:      ensemble.AssignmentDone,
+				AssignedAt:  now,
+				CompletedAt: &now,
+			},
+		},
+	}
+	if err := ensemble.SaveSession(session, state); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] SaveSession failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	sendYAMLModeOutput(t, session, 0, "E2E export findings thesis")
+
+	// Run synthesize with JSON format to get findings.
+	res := runEnsembleUXCmd(t, suite, "synthesize_for_export",
+		"ensemble", "synthesize", session, "--format", "json",
+	)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - stdout: %s", t.Name(), truncateString(string(res.Stdout), 500))
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - exit code err: %v", t.Name(), res.Err)
+
+	if res.Err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] synthesize for export failed: %v", res.Err)
+	}
+
+	// Verify output is valid JSON.
+	var out map[string]interface{}
+	parseEnsembleUXJSON(t, suite, "synthesize_for_export", res.Stdout, &out)
+
+	if _, ok := out["synthesis"]; !ok {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] synthesize output missing 'synthesis' field")
+	}
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: synthesis JSON has findings = true", t.Name())
+}

+
+func TestE2E_Presets_RobotJSON(t *testing.T) {
+	CommonE2EPrerequisites(t)
+
+	if !supportsNTMSubcommand("ensemble") {
+		t.Skip("ntm binary does not support `ensemble` command")
+	}
+
+	suite := NewTestSuite(t, "ensemble_ux_presets_robot")
+	defer suite.Teardown()
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - testing robot ensemble-presets JSON output", t.Name())
+
+	res := runEnsembleUXCmd(t, suite, "robot_presets",
+		"--robot-ensemble-presets",
+	)
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - stdout: %s", t.Name(), truncateString(string(res.Stdout), 500))
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - exit code err: %v", t.Name(), res.Err)
+
+	if res.Err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] --robot-ensemble-presets failed: %v", res.Err)
+	}
+
+	var out presetsListJSON
+	parseEnsembleUXJSON(t, suite, "robot_presets", res.Stdout, &out)
+
+	if out.Count == 0 {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] expected at least 1 preset")
+	}
+	if out.Count != len(out.Presets) {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] preset count mismatch: count=%d len=%d", out.Count, len(out.Presets))
+	}
+
+	t.Logf("E2E: %s - assertion: preset_count = %d (want >0)", t.Name(), out.Count)
+
+	for i, p := range out.Presets {
+		if p.Name == "" {
+			t.Fatalf("[E2E-ENSEMBLE-UX] preset[%d] has empty name", i)
+		}
+		t.Logf("E2E: %s - preset[%d]: name=%s mode_count=%d", t.Name(), i, p.Name, p.ModeCount)
+	}
